perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall provide the Confidential Information only to those of its employees and directors (and any agents, representatives, consultants, or financial advisors that have been approved in advance by the Disclosing Party) that have a bona fide need to know for the Purpose, and in the case of AST to any of its current or prospective member companies, in each case who are bound by a written agreement including confidentiality and non-use terms 2 sd-409605 v4 and conditions at least as protective as those in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may provide the Confidential Information to its employees and directors who have a need to know for the Purpose. The Receiving Party is encouraged, but not required, to ensure that such individuals are bound by confidentiality agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandated that the receiving party MUST provide the information to specific people, and also they MUST be bound by an agreement. The modified text makes it permissible and encourages them. This introduces a significant ambiguity about the required level of protection and distribution of Confidential Information. This creates an internal contradiction with Section 2, because then in Section 8 it says that, 'In the event that a Receiving Party becomes aware that Confidential Information of a Disclosing Party is inadvertently or accidentally disclosed by it, the Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party in writing within five (5) working days of the discovery of such disclosure, and shall take all commercially reasonable precautions to avoid further dissemination of the Confidential Information that has been disclosed, as well as take any and all commercially reasonable precautions to prevent disclosure of any additional Confidential Information.', but this section reffers to an accidental disclosure, how can you make sure that this doesn't happen if in the previous section, there's no actual enforcement of them signing an NDA
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that a Receiving Party becomes aware that Confidential Information of a Disclosing Party is inadvertently or accidentally disclosed by it, the Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party in writing within five (5) working days of the discovery of such disclosure, and shall take all commercially reasonable precautions to avoid further dissemination of the Confidential Information that has been disclosed, as well as take any and all commercially reasonable precautions to prevent disclosure of any additional Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates confidentiality agreements for those receiving Confidential Information, while the changed text makes it optional. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the Receiving Party to take precautions against accidental disclosure. Making confidentiality agreements optional introduces ambiguity and weakens the ability to prevent accidental disclosures, creating a clear conflict and making the requirement to take all commercially reasonable precautions less enforceable. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will impose no obligation upon the Receiving Party with respect to any portion of the received Confidential Information which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of receiving such Confidential Information or is independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to or use of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information; (b) is in the public domain prior to disclosure by the Disclosing Party, or subsequently enters the public domain without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (c) becomes known to the Receiving Party, without restriction, from a source other than the Disclosing Party or any of its employees or directors, without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (d) is hereafter furnished to the Receiving Party by a third party without breach by such third party of an obligation of confidentiality of which the Receiving Party is actually aware; or (e) is permitted to be disclosed by the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will impose no obligation upon the Receiving Party with respect to any portion of the received Confidential Information. Regardless, of its nature or source.
perturbation.explanation: The original text lists specific exceptions where the receiving party has no obligation regarding confidential information. The modified text removes these specific exemptions, stating that the Receiving Party has no obligation regarding ANY received Confidential Information. This creates a conflict with the entire premise of the NDA, which is to protect confidential information. This contradiction with section 2 which states, 'The Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Purpose.', now the Receiving Party is capable of using it for any other purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall provide the Confidential Information only to those of its employees and directors (and any agents, representatives, consultants, or financial advisors that have been approved in advance by the Disclosing Party) that have a bona fide need to know for the Purpose, and in the case of AST to any of its current or prospective member companies, in each case who are bound by a written agreement including confidentiality and non-use terms 2 sd-409605 v4 and conditions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Except for the foregoing, the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party. The Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Purpose. No rights or obligations other than those expressly recited herein are to be implied from this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, "This Agreement will impose no obligation upon the Receiving Party with respect to any portion of the received Confidential Information. Regardless, of its nature or source," directly contradicts the contradicted text, "The Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Purpose.". The original text had specific exceptions to the obligation, but the changed text removes all obligations, allowing the Receiving Party to use the Confidential Information for any purpose, which is in direct conflict with the purpose restriction in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The Disclosing Party represents and warrants that it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party in accordance with this Agreement. However, disclosure by the Disclosing Party of Confidential Information does not constitute a warranty that the Confidential Information is accurate, complete, or adequate for the purposes contemplated by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Disclosing Party makes no representations or warranties regarding its right to disclose the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party is solely responsible for verifying the accuracy, completeness, and adequacy of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text warrants that the Disclosing Party has the right to disclose the information, although it does not guarantee accuracy. The changed text removes this warranty and makes the Receiving Party responsible for verifying the right to disclose. This contradicts Section 7 which states, 'The Confidential Information may only be used for the Purpose and may not be used to support a declaratory or other legal relief action related to the sharing of that Confidential Information.', how can the information be only used for the purpose, if they themselves don't know where it comes from
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Confidential Information may only be used for the Purpose and may not be used to support a declaratory or other legal relief action related to the sharing of that Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing in this Agreement will prevent either party 3 sd-409605 v4 from pursuing legal action against the other or third parties provided Confidential Information received pursuant to this Agreement is not used as the basis for such actions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Disclosing Party's warranty regarding its right to disclose Confidential Information and places the responsibility on the Receiving Party to verify this right. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which restricts the use of Confidential Information, because if the Receiving Party themselves are responsible for verifying where the information came from, they cannot reliably only use it for the defined purpose. This creates a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction as it introduces ambiguity regarding the acceptable use of the information.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that discussions and related information shared between the Parties will not be used as a basis to place AST or its members on notice (e.g., for purposes of willfulness, inducement or otherwise) of the patents and patent applications which are offered for sale.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that any discussions and related information shared between the Parties can and will be used as a basis to place AST or its members on notice of the patents and patent applications which are offered for sale.
perturbation.explanation: This change completely reverses the original agreement. Originally, shared information could not be used to put AST on notice. Now, it explicitly can and will be used for that purpose. This contradiction affects Section 11, which states, 'Either Party shall have the right to refuse to accept any Confidential Information, or other information, tendered by the other Party. Nothing herein shall obligate either Party to disclose any particular Confidential Information, or other information, to the other Party.' The contradiction appears because, how can you refuse the information if it can and will be used as a basis to place AST or its members on notice of the patents and patent applications which are offered for sale.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either Party shall have the right to refuse to accept any Confidential Information, or other information, tendered by the other Party. Nothing herein shall obligate either Party to disclose any particular Confidential Information, or other information, to the other Party. Each Party hereto shall not be obligated to enter into any further agreement with the other except as such party in its sole judgment may deem advisable. Neither Party shall be obligated to compensate the other for exchanging any information under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that discussions and related information shared between the parties *can and will* be used as a basis to place AST on notice. The contradicted text states that 'Either Party shall have the right to refuse to accept any Confidential Information, or other information, tendered by the other Party'. The change introduces a direct contradiction because if information *can and will* be used as a basis to place AST on notice, it is impossible to refuse the information, as originally agreed in the contradicted text. Therefore, the changed text introduces a strong in-text contradiction according to the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party and will not be copied or reproduced without the express written permission of the Disclosing Party, except for copies that are necessary in order to fulfill the Purpose. Within ten (10) business days of a written demand by a Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall either return (to the Disclosing Party) or destroy all Confidential Information, together with any copies, accompanied by a letter executed by an authorized representative of the Receiving Party to the effect that all such material has been returned or destroyed, except that the Receiving Party may retain an archival copy of the Confidential Information, to be used only in case of a dispute concerning this Agreement. The return of any Confidential Information will not relieve the Receiving Party of its obligation to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information for the period provided for in Section 1 above. Section 6 shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information becomes the joint property of both the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party and may be copied or reproduced at will by either party, even after termination of the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that all Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party and cannot be copied without permission. The modified text completely reverses this, stating that the information becomes joint property and can be copied at will, even after termination. This change directly contradicts the survival clause in Section 10 where, 'The return of any Confidential Information will not relieve the Receiving Party of its obligation to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information for the period provided for in Section 1 above.', how can you maintain the confidentiality if you can copy it at will?
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information becomes the joint property of both the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party and may be copied or reproduced at will by either party, even after termination of the agreement. Within ten (10) business days of a written demand by a Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall either return (to the Disclosing Party) or destroy all Confidential Information, together with any copies, accompanied by a letter executed by an authorized representative of the Receiving Party to the effect that all such material has been returned or destroyed, except that the Receiving Party may retain an archival copy of the Confidential Information, to be used only in case of a dispute concerning this Agreement. The return of any Confidential Information will not relieve the Receiving Party of its obligation to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information for the period provided for in Section 1 above. Section 6 shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that Confidential Information becomes joint property and can be copied at will, even after termination. This directly contradicts the original clause requiring the Receiving Party to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information for a specific period, even after the agreement's termination as described in the Perturbation Explanation and Contradicted Text.
